title: Task Panel — Interactive UI
description: Create a TaskPanel class (tkinter Frame) that displays and manages tasks interactively. It contains: a task_list (Listbox or Treeview) showing task titles, an add-task form with Entry fields for title, description, and priority, filter buttons (All, Active, Completed), and a detail area. Methods: refresh_tasks() reloads from TaskDatabase and updates the list, add_task() reads from the form fields and inserts into the database, toggle_status() flips a task between 'active' and 'completed'. The panel connects to a TaskDatabase instance passed in the constructor.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 9: TaskPanel
import sys

assert 'TaskPanel' in dir(), 'TaskPanel class must be defined'

# Try to test with tkinter
try:
    import tkinter as tk
    root = tk.Tk()
    root.withdraw()

    db = TaskDatabase(':memory:')
    db.add_task('Test Task 1', 'Description 1', 'high')
    db.add_task('Test Task 2', 'Description 2', 'low')

    parent = tk.Frame(root)
    panel = TaskPanel(parent, db)

    # Test that panel has required attributes
    assert hasattr(panel, 'task_list'), 'Must have task_list attribute'
    assert hasattr(panel, 'title_entry'), 'Must have title_entry attribute'
    assert hasattr(panel, 'desc_entry'), 'Must have desc_entry attribute'
    assert hasattr(panel, 'priority_entry'), 'Must have priority_entry attribute'
    assert hasattr(panel, 'frame'), 'Must have frame attribute'

    # Test that required methods exist
    assert callable(getattr(panel, 'refresh_tasks', None)), 'Must have refresh_tasks method'
    assert callable(getattr(panel, 'add_task', None)), 'Must have add_task method'
    assert callable(getattr(panel, 'toggle_status', None)), 'Must have toggle_status method'

    # Test refresh_tasks populates the list
    assert panel.task_list.size() >= 2, f'Task list should show 2 tasks, got {panel.task_list.size()}'

    # Test that _tasks cache is populated
    assert hasattr(panel, '_tasks'), 'Must have _tasks cache'
    assert len(panel._tasks) >= 2, 'Should have 2 tasks cached'

    # Test add_task via form
    panel.title_entry.insert(0, 'New Task')
    panel.desc_entry.insert(0, 'New description')
    panel.priority_entry.insert(0, 'medium')
    panel.add_task()
    assert panel.task_list.size() >= 3, 'Should have 3 tasks after adding'

    # Verify task was added to database
    all_tasks = db.get_tasks_by_status('active')
    titles = [t['title'] for t in all_tasks]
    assert 'New Task' in titles, 'New Task should be in database'

    # Test form was cleared after add
    assert panel.title_entry.get() == '', 'Title entry should be cleared after add'

    # Test toggle_status method exists and is callable
    # (Can't easily test selection in headless mode, but method must exist)
    assert callable(panel.toggle_status), 'toggle_status must be callable'

    root.destroy()

except ImportError:
    # tkinter not available, test class structure
    assert callable(getattr(TaskPanel, 'refresh_tasks', None)), 'Must have refresh_tasks'
    assert callable(getattr(TaskPanel, 'add_task', None)), 'Must have add_task'
    assert callable(getattr(TaskPanel, 'toggle_status', None)), 'Must have toggle_status'
acceptance_criteria: TaskPanel creates a Frame inside the parent widget
task_list (Listbox) displays task titles from the database
Form fields: title_entry, desc_entry, priority_entry for new task input
Filter buttons for All, Active, Completed
refresh_tasks() reloads from TaskDatabase and updates the Listbox
add_task() reads form fields, inserts into database, clears form, refreshes
toggle_status() flips selected task between active/completed
_tasks list caches the current task data for selection lookup
hints: Use tk.Listbox for task_list. Insert items with task_list.insert(tk.END, title).
Use tk.Entry for form fields. Read with entry.get(), clear with entry.delete(0, tk.END).
For refresh_tasks, first task_list.delete(0, tk.END) to clear, then insert all titles.
For toggle_status, get selection with task_list.curselection() which returns a tuple of indices.
To get all tasks (no filter), use search_tasks('') which matches everything via LIKE '%%'.
